files.Title: CalloraVoipSdk.Core.Security.SipDomainCertificateValidator
files.Summary:
Validates X.509 certificates against SIP domain identities per RFC 5922 "Domain Certificates in SIP".

RFC 5922 §7.1 requires that when a SIP entity establishes a TLS connection, it MUST verify the server certificate contains a subjectAltName (SAN) extension with a value that matches the expected SIP domain. Two SAN entry types are valid:

  1. uniformResourceIdentifier — a sip: or sips: URI whose host component matches the SIP domain (case-insensitive DNS comparison).
  2. dNSName — a DNS hostname that matches the SIP domain, with wildcard support for the leftmost label (e.g. *.example.com).

This validator is intentionally stateless and purely functional so that it can be used from callback contexts (e.g. certificate validation callbacks) without thread-safety concerns.

files.Title: CalloraVoipSdk.Core.Security.SrtpDecisionReasonCodes
files.Summary: Stable reason-code constants for SRTP policy and negotiation decisions. These values are emitted in call media metadata and SIP telemetry.

files.Title: CalloraVoipSdk.Core.Security.TlsConfiguration
files.Summary:
TLS configuration for SIP transport connections.

Supports both outbound (client) and inbound (server) TLS use-cases. Certificate loading is lazy and cached after the first call to .

RFC 5922 compliance: set to enable domain certificate validation per RFC 5922 §7.1 in addition to the standard chain and hostname checks performed by the TLS stack.
